description:
Advances: specled.tagged_tests

Deliverable:
Full detail: refined plan Stage 3 (mini-plan think-t2d run 20260702-071642). Depends on S2 (specled_-0y8.2). Pure verifier-side addition on S2's artifact.
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex merged_tagged_tests_results/4: single resume pass. Trigger: merged run carries the timeout fact (s65.7) AND artifact was readable AND :not_started remainder non-empty. Build resume command via existing TaggedTests.build_command(remainder_ids, tag_map) — never-started ids ONLY ({:in_flight} hang suspects deliberately NOT re-run; a hanger would just burn the second budget). Run through the same run_command with a FRESH full timeout budget and a second artifact path. Merge attributions via NEW SpecLedEx.TaggedTests.Attribution.merge/2 — first run's recorded outcomes win for observed ids; resume artifact fills the remainder. Findings and strengths computed from the merged map. Exactly one resume pass, no loop. If the resume run also times out: finding says so explicitly, names the resume run's own in-flight set, and calls out a repeated suspect ('this test, not the budget, is the likely problem').
- Skip conditions (each falls back to S2 behavior): no timeout fact; no artifact (degradation); empty remainder; build_command -> :no_tests.
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md: NEW requirements (append-only): specled.tagged_tests.resume_pass_over_remainder (+ scenario ...scenario.resume_runs_remainder_after_timeout) and specled.tagged_tests.resume_double_timeout_signal (+ scenario ...scenario.resume_double_timeout_names_suspect); append cover ids to the tagged_tests verification block. Note: S2's scenario merged_run_executes_once_across_subjects stays valid (resume only triggers on timeout; that shim run is green).
- No ADR change — resume is a consequence of specled.decision.evidence_based_attribution (its Consequences already cover it).
- Regenerate .spec/state.json.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs — shim mix that times out on first invocation while writing a partial artifact, succeeds on second; assert two invocations, second command contains only remainder files, first-run passes keep executed strength; double-timeout variant asserts the repeat-timeout message names suspect test ids. test/specled_ex/tagged_tests/attribution_test.exs — unit tests for Attribution.merge/2 (first-run-wins, remainder-fill), tagged to resume_pass_over_remainder.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: the two scenarios authored in this stage.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests/attribution.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/tagged_tests/attribution_test.exs
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests/formatter.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/**
- test/integration/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- No re-run of failed or in-flight tests; no retry of the resume pass itself; no persistent checkpoint across mix spec.check invocations.
- No build_command/2 or formatter changes.
- No new CLI flags — budget comes from existing command_timeout_ms / --command-timeout-ms.
- No new finding code for double timeout — message text only.
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer; @impl with explicit behaviour module; escape interpolation in doc strings.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-specled-0y8-3 (subagent agent-aimpl-specled-0y8-3-83d3f1f8b8a0633e)
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/3eb68c45-877b-4c94-b9df-996b5269db42/subagents/agent-aimpl-specled-0y8-3-83d3f1f8b8a0633e.jsonl

Commit: 6a65f84
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests/attribution.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/tagged_tests/attribution_test.exs
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- .spec/state.json
Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.TaggedTests.AttributionTest "merge/2": first-run-wins over resume; resume fills the never-started remainder; single-side covers carried through; empty resume leaves first run untouched (4 tests, tagged resume_pass_over_remainder)
- SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "a merged-run timeout resumes the never-started remainder and fills it green" (tagged resume_pass_over_remainder)
- SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "a resume pass that also times out names the repeated suspect, not the budget" (tagged resume_double_timeout_signal)
Verification stubs flipped: none — the tagged_tests verification block was already execute:true; the two new requirements' covers were appended to that existing executing block.
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Added a single resume pass in Verifier.merged_tagged_tests_results/4 (maybe_resume_after_timeout/5 + resumed_result/4) that, after a merged-run timeout with a readable artifact and a non-empty :not_started remainder, re-runs only the never-started cover ids via TaggedTests.build_command/2 with a fresh full budget and a second artifact, then folds the outcomes in through the new Attribution.merge/2 (first-run evidence wins for observed covers; resume fills the remainder). In-flight hang suspects are never re-run and the resume is never itself resumed; when the resume also times out, attributed_timeout_details appends a signal naming the resume's own in-flight tests and stating the test, not the budget, is the likely problem. All four skip conditions fall back to the S2 single-run behavior unchanged.

Notes for verify/audit:
- Verified against the epic base: `mix spec.check --base 8044a74` is green (status=pass, errors=0), stable across 3 runs. Default base=origin/main shows unrelated append_only findings per the epic's known-drift note; 8044a74 is the correct base for this diff.
- Merge gates run clean: mix compile --warnings-as-errors; mix test (857 tests, 0 failures); mix format --check-formatted (exit 0, confirmed directly); mix deps.unlock --check-unused (exit 0).
- The resume trigger legitimately fires for the two pre-existing timeout tests (hang-suspects, compile-cost) since they leave a non-empty never-started remainder. Their assertions still hold, but the hang-suspects test now double-runs; I bumped its command_timeout_ms 300 -> 2000 (and wrote the new shims to emit their artifact as the first action) so the first-run artifact write reliably lands within budget under the loaded merged run — the tight 300ms budget was the cause of a flaky misclassification in the full merged `mix test`.
- Branch guard: verifier.ex/verifier_test.exs sit in the surface of prose_guard/decisions/verification; the change alters none of their contracts and needs no new ADR (consequence of the existing specled.decision.evidence_based_attribution). Attested via `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info, branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info` (same mechanism S1/S2 used); the findings remain visible as info.

title: S3: Resume pass over never-started covers after merged-run timeout
